name: Irish English (Hiberno-English)

ancestors.notes: Irish Gaelic substrate: dental stops for θ/ð; clear l; intonation patterns; some syntax

notes: Hiberno-English / Irish English. Rhotic. /θ/ → [t̪] and /ð/ → [d̪] (dental stops — Gaelic substrate). Clear [l] everywhere (no dark l). GOAT/FACE often monophthongs [oː/eː]. Irish Gaelic substrate: significant lexical influence. Source: Wells (1982) vol. 2.

sources.author: Wells, J.C.
sources.title: Accents of English, Vol. 2: The British Isles

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Irish English.
